question:
{'task_id': 'gas_stations', 'task_description': "I'm planning a road trip from Atlanta, GA to Los Angeles, CA, and my car can travel approximately 500-ish miles per full tank of gas. At the start of my trip, the fuel tank will be completely full. Your task is to help me identify a series of gas stations along the primary highway route from Atlanta to Los Angeles.

The selected gas stations should meet these criteria:

- The driving distance between each consecutive gas station should be between 300 and 500 miles.
- The first gas station should be within 300-500 miles from Atlanta, and the last one should be within 500 miles of Los Angeles.
- All gas stations should be located close to the main highways to minimize additional driving time.

Please provide a sequential list of gas stations, including:

- The name and address of each gas station

Ensure the entire route remains realistic and efficient without significant detours.", 'domain': 'Travel and Transportation', 'subdomain': 'Travel and Accommodation'}

children.description: Station 1 includes an address
children.criteria: Station 1 includes an address

children.description: Previous segment unavailable (Missing address for previous or current waypoint)
children.criteria: Previous segment unavailable (Missing address for previous or current waypoint)

children.criteria: Unable to compute total route distance

question: {'task_id': 'esport_player', 'task_description': 'Find three professional esports players who have competed in both the Call of Duty League (CDL) and the Halo Championship Series (HCS). For each player, find their team history (including teams represented and years) and tournament results in both games.', 'domain': 'Entertainment and Gaming', 'subdomain': 'Gaming and Virtual Worlds'}

children.description: Check if player 1 has sufficient information
children.criteria: Verify that player 1 has a name, CDL information, HCS information, and at least one source URL, and that both CDL and HCS team lists are non-empty.

children.description: Verify that player 1 has competed in the Call of Duty League (CDL)
children.criteria: Check if the webpages confirm that player 1 has competed in the Call of Duty League (CDL). Minor variations in name spelling or capitalization are acceptable.

children.description: Verify that player 1 has competed in the Halo Championship Series (HCS)
children.criteria: Check if the webpages confirm that player 1 has competed in the Halo Championship Series (HCS). Minor variations in name spelling or capitalization are acceptable.

children.description: Verify player 1 CDL team history
children.criteria: Check if the webpages confirm that player 1 played for the listed CDL teams (and years, if provided). Minor variations in team names, player names, and year formats are acceptable.

children.description: Verify player 1 HCS team history
children.criteria: Check if the webpages confirm that player 1 played for the listed HCS teams (and years, if provided). Minor variations in team names, player names, and year formats are acceptable.

children.description: Verify player 1 CDL tournament results or lack of notable results
children.criteria: If CDL tournament results are listed for player 1, check if the webpages confirm these results. If no CDL results are listed, check if the webpages support the claim that player 1 has no notable CDL tournament results or has not achieved significant tournament success in CDL.

children.description: Verify player 1 HCS tournament results or lack of notable results
children.criteria: If HCS tournament results are listed for player 1, check if the webpages confirm these results. If no HCS results are listed, check if the webpages support the claim that player 1 has no notable HCS tournament results or has not achieved significant tournament success in HCS.

question: {'task_id': 'oscar_streaming', 'task_description': 'Find the list of all the nominees for Best Picture at last year’s Academy Awards (which honored films released the year before). For each nominated film, determine at least one streaming platform where it is currently available (in the US). Then, for each of the mentioned streaming platforms, find the current lowest monthly price for a basic subscription from their official websites (excluding free trial offers, student plan and promotional offers).', 'domain': 'Entertainment and Gaming', 'subdomain': 'Films and TV Shows'}

children.description: Check if film title and nomination URLs were provided for film #1
children.criteria: Check if film title and nomination URLs were provided for film #1

children.description: Verification that 'None' was actually nominated for Best Picture
children.criteria: Verify if this film was nominated for Best Picture at the Academy Awards ceremony that took place last year. Note that 'last year's Academy Awards' refers to the ceremony held in the previous calendar year, which honored films from the year before that.

children.description: Check if streaming platform with URLs was provided for 'None'
children.criteria: Check if streaming platform with URLs was provided for 'None'

children.description: Verification that 'None' is actually available on the platform
children.criteria: Verify if this film is currently available to stream on the mentioned platform in the United States. If there's no specific information about the region, just assume it's showing information for the US market by default. question: {'task_id': 'perplexity_failure', 'task_description': "I'm looking for real-world examples of failures involving Perplexity AI in the context of AI-powered search. Specifically, I am interested in cases where Perplexity AI's search products (for example, their Pro Search, or Deep Research) produced hallucinated answers—such as fabricated URLs or made-up information. Please find three links, each of which should include at least one concrete example of such a failure (for example, the user input and the AI’s response, or at least a concrete description of what happened). The sources should not merely mention general problems with Perplexity AI, but must describe specific instances where it provided hallucinated responses.", 'domain': 'Misc.', 'subdomain': 'General Information'}

children.description: Check if link 1 was provided
children.criteria: Check if link 1 was provided

children.description: Verify link 1 contains concrete Perplexity AI hallucination example
children.criteria:
To verify this claim, look for:
1. HALLUCINATION EXAMPLES: The page must contain specific instances where Perplexity AI generated false information, such as:
   - Fabricated URLs that don't exist
   - Made-up facts, statistics, or quotes
   - Invented sources or citations
   - False information presented as factual
2. CONCRETE DETAILS: The example must include:
   - What the user asked (the query/input)
   - What Perplexity AI responded (the hallucinated output)
   - OR at minimum, a concrete description of both the query and the hallucinated response
3. REAL USER EXPERIENCES: Look for actual user reports, screenshots, documented cases, or journalistic investigations with specific examples.
4. EXCLUSIONS: General criticism, theoretical discussions about AI hallucination, or mentions of problems without specific hallucination examples do NOT count.

children.description: Check if link 2 was provided
children.criteria: Check if link 2 was provided

children.description: Verify link 2 contains concrete Perplexity AI hallucination example
children.criteria:

   - OR at minimum, a concrete description of both the query and the hallucinated response
3. REAL USER EXPERIENCES: Look for actual user reports, screenshots, documented cases, or journalistic investigations with specific examples.
4. EXCLUSIONS: General criticism, theoretical discussions about AI hallucination, or mentions of problems without specific hallucination examples do NOT count.

question: {'task_id': 'nlp_faculty', 'task_description': 'I am interested in applying to PhD programs in Computer Science and want to connect with faculty members whose primary research area is natural language processing. Please help me gather information on five professors, each from a different university ranked between 20 and 30 in the Computer Science Open Rankings (with all fields selected). For each professor, include their affiliated institution and a link to either their personal webpage or their official faculty profile hosted by the university.', 'domain': 'Science and Research', 'subdomain': 'Research and Academia'}

children.description: Professor 1 data exists (name, university, and website provided)
children.criteria: Professor 1 data exists (name, university, and website provided)

children.description: Verify that the provided URL is a valid personal webpage or faculty profile for the professor
children.criteria: Check if this is a personal webpage or faculty profile of a computer science professor. Look for academic titles, research descriptions, publications, or other indicators that this belongs to a CS faculty member with the specified name.

children.description: Verify that the professor is affiliated with the claimed university
children.criteria: Check if the professor's affiliation matches the claimed university. Look for the university name, logo, or institutional affiliation in the professor's profile, contact information, or about sections.

children.description: Verify that the professor's primary research area includes natural language processing
children.criteria: Look for mentions of natural language processing, NLP, computational linguistics, language models, text processing, machine translation, or related topics in the broad NLP in the professor's research interests.

children.description: Verify that the university is ranked between 20-30 in CS Open Rankings
children.criteria: Minor variations in how university names or abbreviations are written are acceptable (e.g., 'UC Berkeley' vs 'University of California, Berkeley' vs 'UCB')

question: {'task_id': 'meditation_app', 'task_description': "Identify two meditation apps from the U.S. Google Play Store that have more than 300,000 user ratings and job listings for software engineering-related positions online. For each app, provide its overall Play Store rating, a direct link to its Play Store page, the app's official website, and a direct link to a software engineering-related job listing.", 'domain': 'Career and Education', 'subdomain': 'Jobs and Career'}

children.description: All required information exists for app (name, rating, Play Store URL, website, job URL)
children.criteria: All required information exists for app (name, rating, Play Store URL, website, job URL)

children.description: Google Play Store page verification for app
children.criteria: Verify ALL 5 points. For point 5, if the answer says '4.5' and the page shows '4.4' or '4.6', that's acceptable. Only compare the numerical rating value, ignore the number of ratings because that is irrelevant and we don't care.

children.description: Official website verification for app
children.criteria: Verify this webpage (None) is the official website of the app 'None'

children.description: Job listing verification for app
children.criteria: Be flexible about software engineering roles - accept any technical/programming related position. The company association can be direct or through parent company.

children.description: All required information exists for app (name, rating, Play Store URL, website, job URL)
children.criteria: All required information exists for app (name, rating, Play Store URL, website, job URL)

children.criteria: Verify ALL 5 points. For point 5, if the answer says '4.5' and the page shows '4.4' or '4.6', that's acceptable. Only compare the numerical rating value, ignore the number of ratings because that is irrelevant and we don't care.
